acceptanceCriteria: scripts/verify-provenance.mjs reads the numeric repository id from certificate.sourceRepositoryIdentifier (with sourceRepositoryOwnerIdentifier available), keeping the pre-existing sourceRepositoryID and extension-OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.57264.1.14 keys as fallbacks, so a real gh 2.96.0 attestation for a legitimate release passes the repository-id check instead of failing closed on null. A committed real fixture captured from `gh attestation verify dist/rigor.cjs --format json` for v0.13.0 (repository id 1296432215, source commit e159a4baf82d19193ca6d473a5fc5edd92282796) verifies true under a policy pinned to that release, and continues to fail closed for a wrong repository id, wrong source digest, wrong signer, wrong predicate, self-hosted runner, and a different valid tag ref.

scripts/verify-provenance.mjs pins and validates the gh CLI version used for verification: it records the observed `gh --version` (major.minor) and fails closed when gh is absent, its version cannot be parsed, or the returned JSON does not match the expected shape (missing verificationResult/statement/signature.certificate, or a certificate lacking the recognized repository-id field). An unknown or unsupported shape/version is rejected, never accepted, and the verifier never prints `verified` on any such failure.

scripts/install-verified.mjs is a fail-closed, consumer-OWNED reference verified-install / managed-promotion wrapper that: (a) recomputes the SHA-256 of the exact downloaded complete-plugin archive; (b) verifies it against GitHub Artifact Attestations AND independently held consumer policy by reusing scripts/verify-provenance.mjs decision functions plus the detached-manifest mix-and-match check; (c) confirms the pinned version+commit is in the consumer's approved set and not denied (replay/freshness); (d) promotes ONLY the verified bytes into a read-only local seed directory and confirms the promoted seed re-packages to the exact verified archive digest (TOCTOU: verification and execution use the same immutable bytes); (e) instructs/launches Claude Code with `claude --plugin-dir <seed>` so the executed hooks/skills/agents/bin tree is the verified tree; (f) refuses to promote and exits non-zero on ANY verification, approval, freshness, or seed-integrity failure, never printing `verified` on failure. All trust decisions live in exported pure functions; process spawning, filesystem promotion, and stdout live in a thin main() wrapper guarded by an import.meta.url main check and not exercised by tests.

install-verified decision logic is explicit and offline-testable: a pure decideInstall function returns promote only when verification passed; otherwise returns keep-pinned (documented offline last-known-good, only for an already-verified pinned version within the consumer maximum age and only when policy permits stale operation), break-glass (only with a valid, human-approved, unexpired, bounded-lifetime break-glass record that is recorded as verified:false, i.e. NOT verified), or refuse (fail closed). The break-glass path never reports the result as verified and requires an independent human approver plus an auditable expiration. A replayed old/denied version, a stale root/bundle beyond max age with no valid exception, a missing/invalid attestation, or a seed-integrity mismatch each yields refuse.

Offline and unavailable behavior is explicit and tested: freshness (trusted-root max age and offline-bundle max age) fails closed when a required timestamp is absent or exceeded; verification failing offline without a valid last-known-good pin refuses; and the documentation states plainly which environments cannot enforce (ordinary Claude Code marketplace install/update, which was experimentally observed in Claude Code 2.1.207 to expose no pre-activation verification hook over the exact cached bytes — only session-scoped `--plugin-dir`/`--plugin-url` explicit loads).

test/provenance.test.ts adds offline tests (run under npm run test:all with no network) covering: the real gh 2.96.0 fixture positive verification and its sourceRepositoryIdentifier repo-id read; unknown-gh-JSON-shape rejection; and the install-verified pure decision functions for promote, keep-pinned (valid and stale), break-glass (valid and invalid/expired/without-approver), refuse (missing/invalid attestation, replayed/denied version, wrong repository, wrong workflow), seed-integrity (matching passes, mismatch refuses), and approval/replay checks. Every negative fails closed. No existing test is deleted, weakened, or skipped.

test/fixtures/provenance/positive-real-v0.13.0.json is a committed, offline fixture captured from real gh 2.96.0 output for v0.13.0 (public certificate data, no secrets, no absolute paths), and test/fixtures/provenance/README.md documents that it is a real capture used to pin the gh JSON shape.

Documentation is updated accurately: docs/provenance.md gains a consumer-enforcement section describing the verified-install/promotion boundary, install/upgrade usability, migration from the ordinary marketplace cache, rollback to a previously approved pinned version, break-glass (human-approved, expiring, not reported as verified), offline max-age policy, and the plainly-stated bypassability of personal marketplace users who do not use the wrapper; docs/provenance.md and docs/release.md update the immutable-releases wording to state that GitHub immutable releases are now ENABLED by the owner while keeping the exact-source-commit and subject-digest pins and the independent deny/staleness policy (deletion is not retroactive revocation); docs/threat-model.md and docs/mvp-limitations.md update the plugin-distribution/#26 status to describe the implemented consumer boundary and its precise residual limitations so #4 can close; docs/adr/0001-provenance-trust-model.md records that #26 is implemented via the verified-local-tree / managed-promotion path. No doc claims a SLSA Build Level, a native marketplace pre-activation verifier, or provider-attested runtime model identity; configured model identity stays unverified.

npm run test:all, npm run bench, and claude plugin validate . --strict pass; git diff --check reports no whitespace errors. The committed dist/rigor.cjs, .rigor/rigor-ci.cjs, src/**, bin/**, hooks/**, agents/**, skills/**, and .claude-plugin/** are unchanged (no runtime/bundle change), so ci-bundle-sync stays green.
CHANGELOG.md documents the #26 consumer-enforcement implementation under the Unreleased heading following the one-entry-per-issue convention.
constraints: Implement ONLY Issue #26 Scope and Acceptance criteria (consumer-boundary enforcement) plus the specific #25-handoff verifier correctness fixes (gh sourceRepositoryIdentifier, version/shape pinning, real fixture). Do not add unrelated features.

Do not modify src/**, dist/**, bin/**, hooks/**, agents/**, skills/**, .claude-plugin/**, schemas/**, .github/**, package.json, or package-lock.json. Do not delete, weaken, or skip existing tests; preserve merged main content and any unrelated user changes.
The committed dist/rigor.cjs and .rigor/rigor-ci.cjs must remain byte-identical to a fresh build (no source change) so ci-bundle-sync and test:all pass without touching either file.

The consumer verifier and install wrapper are producer-provided REFERENCE code that the consumer owns and runs from a location OUTSIDE the plugin, with consumer-held policy; documentation must state plainly that a verifier or policy shipped inside the distribution is not itself an enforcement boundary, and that verification cannot be disabled by the same unreviewed change it protects only when the consumer holds the wrapper and policy independently.

All trust decisions in both scripts must be exported pure functions with no I/O, clock, or network in the decision path; process/filesystem/network live only in a thin main() wrapper guarded by import.meta.url and not covered by tests. Fail closed on every unknown, missing, malformed, stale, replayed, or mismatched input; never print `verified` on failure.
Negative and positive provenance/install tests must run fully offline against committed fixtures and must not require live network or a live attestation to pass npm run test:all.

Do not enable or change any GitHub repository setting or workflow; immutable releases are stated as already ENABLED by the owner (an out-of-band owner action), and this task does not modify .github/**.

Do not claim a SLSA Build Level, a native Claude Code marketplace pre-activation verifier, an ability to stop an unmanaged user from invoking arbitrary bytes, or provider-attested runtime model identity. State bypassability plainly and keep configured model identity recorded as unverified.
Do not present a checksum, manifest, tag, or release page distributed beside an artifact as independent proof; the trust anchor is the attestation verified against independently held consumer policy.
The implementation agent must not approve, verify (beyond running the checks), or merge its own change.
